profile.behavior: Males perch conspicuously and display their shoulder patches while singing; females stay lower in dense vegetation. Forages mainly on the ground or among plants, and often forms large mixed flocks outside the breeding season.
field_marks: Stocky, broad-shouldered blackbird with a slender, pointed conical bill.
Adult male is glossy black with a vivid red shoulder patch usually edged yellow.
Male shoulder patch can be partly concealed or raised prominently in display.
Female and immature are dark brown and heavily streaked, with paler underparts and a pale eyebrow.
Medium-length, squared-to-rounded tail; dark legs and feet.
profile.habitat: Marshes and wetland edges, including fresh or saltwater marshes; also wet meadows, brushy swamps, fields, and waterside vegetation. Outside the breeding season, commonly uses open agricultural land, pastures, and grassland.

sources.title: Cornell Lab of Ornithology — Red-winged Blackbird Identification
Cornell Lab of Ornithology — Red-winged Blackbird Life History
National Audubon Society — Red-winged Blackbird Field Guide

findings: Independent verification confirms the title, scientific name, family, measurements, behavior, habitat, and listed field marks. Cornell gives the exact displayed measurement ranges; Audubon gives closely matching rounded ranges.

The central bird is a plausible adult male Red-winged Blackbird: glossy black plumage, stocky broad-shouldered form, pointed conical bill, dark eye, medium squared-to-rounded tail, dark legs/feet, and a red epaulet with yellow lower border all agree with the supplied field references and verified descriptions.

No material text or species-identification error found. The bill and feet are somewhat stylized, and only the near wing is visible in the side-on central pose, but the anatomy is coherent: one head, one beak, two legs, one tail, and an implied far wing behind the body.
No place name, ZIP code, coordinates, map, or local-observation detail appears on the plate.
